---
title: "Pipeline Overview"
---

> **Status**: Verified against repo commit `2fa6944` (2026-03-09)
> **See also**: [Epoch QA](epoch-qa.qmd) | [Outputs and Artifacts](outputs-and-artifacts.qmd)

## What this pipeline does

The DSA-110 continuum imaging pipeline converts raw drift-scan HDF5 visibilities
into calibrated images, mosaics, forced photometry catalogs, and (eventually)
multi-epoch light curves. It targets transient and variable source detection at
1.4 GHz using the 110-antenna Deep Synoptic Array at OVRO.

This repository (`dsa110-continuum`) is a clean reimplementation of the older
`dsa110-contimg` codebase. As of Phase 1 (2026-03-10), 450 `dsa110_contimg.core.*`
imports across 136 module files have been replaced with `dsa110_continuum.*` imports.
The remaining ~80 stale references are intentionally preserved in `__init__.py`
re-export layers (prevents double job-type registration from the old package's
bootstrap chain). One inline import in `imaging/worker.py` has no local equivalent.
New development uses `dsa110_continuum.*` imports exclusively.

---

## Pipeline stages

The main production path runs through `batch_pipeline.py`, which processes a
single date end-to-end. `mosaic_day.py` is an alternative standalone script
that produces full-day mosaics grouped by RA strip rather than 1-hour epochs.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    subgraph Input
        HDF5[/"Raw HDF5 files\n/data/incoming/"/]
        DB[("Pipeline SQLite DB")]
    end

    subgraph "Conversion (old CLI)"
        IDX["Index into DB"]
        CONV["HDF5 → MS"]
    end

    subgraph "Per-tile processing (mosaic_day.py)"
        PS["Phaseshift to\nmedian meridian"]
        CAL["Apply B + G\ncalibration"]
        IMG["WSClean imaging\n(pbcor)"]
    end

    subgraph "Epoch assembly (batch_pipeline.py)"
        GCAL["Epoch gaincal\n(optional)"]
        BIN["Bin tiles into\n1-hour epochs"]
        COADD["Noise-weighted\nmosaic"]
    end

    subgraph "Per-epoch analysis"
        PHOT["Forced photometry\n(NVSS, ≥ 10 mJy)"]
        QA["Three-gate\nepoch QA\n(NVSS, ≥ 50 mJy)"]
        PNG["Diagnostic PNG"]
    end

    subgraph "Downstream (standalone scripts)"
        SF["Source finding\n(BANE + Aegean)"]
        LC["Light curve stacking\n+ variability metrics"]
    end

    HDF5 --> IDX --> DB --> CONV
    CONV --> PS --> CAL --> IMG
    IMG --> GCAL --> BIN --> COADD
    COADD --> PHOT
    COADD --> QA --> PNG
    PHOT --> LC
    QA -->|QA-FAIL| REJECT["Epoch logged,\nnot used downstream"]

    style QA fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#4ec9b0
    style REJECT fill:#4a2222,stroke:#e74c3c
```

::: {.callout-note}
- Forced photometry and epoch QA run independently on each epoch mosaic — they are not sequential.
- Source finding (BANE + Aegean) is a separate standalone step, not wired into `batch_pipeline.py`.
- Light curve stacking scripts exist but have not been run on multi-epoch data *(Planned)*.
:::

---

## Stage details

### 1. Conversion (HDF5 → Measurement Set)

- **Script**: `dsa110 convert` (old CLI) / `dsa110_continuum/conversion/`
- **Input**: Raw UVH5 HDF5 files in `/data/incoming/`, grouped by timestamp and subband (16 subbands per observation)
- **Output**: One Measurement Set per observation timestamp in `/stage/dsa110-contimg/ms/`
- **Key detail**: New dates must be indexed into the pipeline SQLite DB first via `dsa110 index add`

### 2. Phaseshift

- **Code**: `mosaic_day.py:process_ms()` → `phaseshift_ms(mode="median_meridian")`
- **Input**: Raw MS
- **Output**: `*_meridian.ms` (phase-rotated to the median meridian of the drift scan)
- **Cleanup**: Meridian MS is deleted after successful imaging unless `--keep-intermediates` is set
- **Silent failure risk**: `FIELD::PHASE_DIR` not updated by `chgcentre` — must be explicitly patched (see [Silent failure risks](#known-silent-failure-risks) below)

### 3. Calibration

- **Code**: `mosaic_day.py:process_ms()` → `apply_to_target()`
- **Input**: Meridian MS + bandpass table (`.b`) + gain table (`.g`)
- **Output**: `CORRECTED_DATA` column populated in the meridian MS
- **Interpolation**: bandpass = `nearest`, gain = `linear`
- **Skip logic**: Calibration is skipped if `CORRECTED_DATA` already exists and its amplitude ratio to `DATA` exceeds 5.0
- **Cal tables**: Currently only Dec ≈ +16° strip has validated tables (from 2026-01-25). Other dates must symlink to these
- **Dec-strip guard**: `batch_pipeline.py` aborts if observed Dec differs from expected cal-table strip by > 5°. Cross-strip application is confirmed to produce near-zero flux (median ratio ≈ 0.06)

**Epoch gaincal** *(Verified)*: When run via `batch_pipeline.py`, a per-epoch gain calibration step optionally precedes tile processing. It runs a short-timescale phase solve (`solint='60s'`) as a pre-conditioner, then a full amplitude+phase solve. If the gain table flagging fraction exceeds 30% (`GAINCAL_FLAG_FRACTION_LIMIT`), the pipeline falls back to the static bandpass-only calibration. Controlled by `--skip-epoch-gaincal`.

### 4. Imaging

- **Code**: `mosaic_day.py:process_ms()` → `image_ms()` (WSClean backend)
- **Input**: Calibrated meridian MS
- **Output**: Per-tile FITS images: `*-image-pb.fits` (primary-beam-corrected, canonical), `*-image.fits` (uncorrected, fallback), `*-beam-0.fits` (beam model)
- **Parameters**: 2400×2400 px, 6″ pixels, Briggs robust=0.5, niter=1000, threshold=5 mJy, `gridder="wgridder"`, `pbcor=True`
- **Silent failure risk**: `TELESCOPE_NAME` must be `DSA_110` before each WSClean run; SPW-merge resets it to `OVRO_MMA`. The wrong beam model causes ~20% photometric error at field edges. Handled automatically inside `run_wsclean()` but must be preserved if the imaging workflow is modified

### 5. Mosaicking

Two modes exist:

| Mode | Script | Grouping | Output |
|------|--------|----------|--------|
| **Batch (primary)** | `batch_pipeline.py` | 1-hour epochs by UTC hour, ±2 tile overlap between adjacent epochs | `{date}T{HH}00_mosaic.fits` |
| **Standalone** | `mosaic_day.py` | RA strips (10° gap threshold, circular-mean RA) | `full_mosaic.fits` or `full_mosaic_ra{NNN}.fits` |

Both modes share the same underlying functions (`build_common_wcs`, `coadd_tiles`):

- **WCS construction**: Circular-mean RA (via `atan2`) to handle the 0°/360° wrap correctly
- **Coaddition**: Noise-weighted (`1/σ²`) using `reproject_interp` onto the common grid
- **PB cutoff**: Pixels where the WSClean beam model is below 20% of peak response are blanked before coadd

::: {.callout-warning}
**Current limitation**: `reproject_interp` allocates a full output-grid array per tile (~500 Mpx), making mosaicking slow (~15 min/tile on large grids). A fix using `reproject_and_coadd` with lazy footprints is planned (Phase 1 in `TODOS.md`).
:::

### 6. Epoch QA

- **Module**: `dsa110_continuum/photometry/epoch_qa.py`
- **Input**: Epoch mosaic FITS + NVSS SQLite catalog
- **Output**: `EpochQAResult` dataclass, diagnostic PNG, row appended to `products/qa_summary.csv`
- **Detail**: See [Epoch QA](epoch-qa.qmd) for gate definitions, thresholds, and constants

### 7. Source finding

- **Script**: `scripts/source_finding.py` (standalone, not wired into batch pipeline)
- **Tools**: BANE (background estimation, 600 px box, 300 px step) + Aegean (7σ detection, 6σ flood-fill)
- **Output**: Source catalog FITS (`*_sources.fits`), background map, RMS map
- **Status**: *Verified* — works on single mosaics

### 8. Forced photometry

Two implementations exist:

| Implementation | Script | Min flux | Purpose |
|---------------|--------|----------|---------|
| **Batch inline** | `batch_pipeline.py` → `measure_forced_peak()` | 10 mJy | Per-epoch flux catalog |
| **Standalone** | `scripts/forced_photometry.py` | 50 mJy | Cross-match with Aegean detections |

A third path inside `epoch_qa.py` uses NVSS ≥ 50 mJy sources — see [Epoch QA](epoch-qa.qmd).

### 9. Source verification

- **Script**: `scripts/verify_sources.py`
- **Catalogs**: `master_sources.sqlite3`, `nvss_full.sqlite3`, `atnf_full.sqlite3` (with deduplication)
- **QA verdict**: PASS (median ratio ≥ 0.80), WARN (≥ 0.70), FAIL (< 0.70 or < 3 detections)

### 10. Light curve extraction *(Planned)*

- **Scripts**: `scripts/stack_lightcurves.py`, `scripts/variability_metrics.py`
- **Status**: Scripts exist but have not been run on multi-epoch data. Blocked on multi-epoch production (Phase 2 in `TODOS.md`)

---

## Known silent-failure risks

These produce no runtime error but yield incorrect science output. All three are
documented in `CLAUDE.md` and must be preserved in any refactoring.

| # | Issue | Guard function | Symptom if missing |
|---|-------|---------------|--------------------|
| 1 | `FIELD::PHASE_DIR` not updated by `chgcentre` | `update_phase_dir_to_target()` | Smeared/offset sources |
| 2 | `FIELD::REFERENCE_DIR` out of sync with `PHASE_DIR` | `sync_reference_dir_with_phase_dir()` | Self-cal divergence, wrong sky model position |
| 3 | `TELESCOPE_NAME` reset to `OVRO_MMA` by SPW-merge | `set_ms_telescope_name("DSA_110")` inside `run_wsclean()` | Wrong EveryBeam model, ~20% photometry error at field edges |

---

## What is implemented vs planned

| Component | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| Conversion (HDF5 → MS) | *Verified* — uses old `dsa110_contimg` CLI |
| Per-tile calibrate + image | *Verified* — single-date, Dec +16° strip |
| Epoch gaincal with BP-only fallback | *Verified* — `GAINCAL_FLAG_FRACTION_LIMIT=0.30` |
| Epoch mosaicking (1-hour bins) | *Verified* — slow (performance fix planned) |
| Full-day mosaicking (RA strips) | *Verified* — `mosaic_day.py` standalone |
| Three-gate epoch QA | *Verified* — tested on 2026-01-25; not yet run at scale |
| Source finding (BANE + Aegean) | *Verified* — single mosaic |
| Forced photometry (single epoch) | *Verified* — both inline and standalone |
| Source verification (`verify_sources.py`) | *Verified* |
| Canary regression test | *Verified* — `run_canary.sh` |
| Multi-epoch batch processing | *Exists* — `batch_run_all_dates.sh` skeleton, not production-tested |
| Per-date calibration automation | *Not started* — manual symlink only (Phase 2) |
| Light curve stacking | *Exists* — scripts written, not yet run at scale |
| Variability metrics | *Exists* — scripts written, not yet run at scale |
| Dec +33° / +54° strip calibration | *Not started* — blocked on cal-table derivation |
| Import rename (`dsa110_contimg` → `dsa110_continuum`) | *Complete (Phase 1)* — 450 replaced; ~80 intentionally preserved in `__init__.py` layers |

---

## Entry points

| Script | Purpose | Typical invocation |
|--------|---------|-------------------|
| `scripts/batch_pipeline.py` | **Primary**: tiles → epoch mosaics → QA → photometry | `python scripts/batch_pipeline.py --date 2026-01-25 --cal-date 2026-01-25` |
| `scripts/mosaic_day.py` | Full-day mosaic by RA strip | `python scripts/mosaic_day.py --date 2026-01-25` |
| `scripts/run_pipeline.py` | Single hardcoded tile (3C454.3, 2026-01-25) | `python scripts/run_pipeline.py` |
| `scripts/run_canary.sh` | Quick regression test (< 1 min) | `bash scripts/run_canary.sh` |
| `scripts/source_finding.py` | BANE + Aegean on a mosaic | `python scripts/source_finding.py --mosaic /path/to.fits` |
| `scripts/forced_photometry.py` | Forced photometry with Aegean cross-match | `python scripts/forced_photometry.py --mosaic /path/to.fits` |
| `scripts/verify_sources.py` | Catalog-driven source verification | `python scripts/verify_sources.py --fits /path/to.fits` |
| `scripts/validate_pipeline.py` | Post-run validation report | `python scripts/validate_pipeline.py --date 2026-01-25` |
| `scripts/inventory.py` | Data inventory (HDF5 + MS census) | `python scripts/inventory.py` |

All scripts require `/opt/miniforge/envs/casa6/bin/python`. Do **not** use system `python3`.
